item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the images appear to be from here and here, with no indication they've been released under a license compliant with the wiki's (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, they have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The testing may have gone in a similar ballpark to what I was expecting, but the approach to it was done well. This is a pretty freaky liquid, to the point where I wonder if it's not just part of an actual species.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like there are a few issues with tense…present-tense when past would make more sense, and future indicative (I hope someone knows what I mean by that) when several other tenses would be better…

Also, I had to read this twice before I really understood what I was reading, but that was probably me being half-asleep the first time. Nit-picks aside, I love this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 152 HV30 appears to be about as hard as stainless steal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All it does is turn into people and have similar personality responses. Hardly end of world destruction or stupid. I personally can't see the over powered in this.

OP; Good idea and I like it a lot, Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The test log felt entirely too… generic-overpowered-SCP to me. I liked the potential from the description, but the test log kinda let me down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The test log really makes this, with all the weird extra effects involved by introducing multiple people to it. The third one was what turned my opinion from "Meh" to "Mreh!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I added something to that effect, since yes, two samples extracted and then unwittingly combined or placed too close to each other could be dangerous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"… nor may samples be combined to form a total volume greater than 2.5 L."

Add that, then I'll upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough. I've updated containment to include these details.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but I'm not upvoting until you fix the containment procedures.

If this thing activates whenever a human gets within (range), then the containment procedures should damn well state that no humans are allowed within (range) except for approved experiments. And if samples of the glop activate whenever they're more than 2.8 L worth, then the containment procedures should damn well state that samples of goop are not allowed to be more than 2.5 L worth except for approved experiments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one just tickles me. I especially love the way it got chatty when it didn't have to actually speak, and wanted to play chess. How much of that is imprinted from the test subject and how much is indicative of this just being friendly (unless externally influenced by crazy guys) yet terribly caustic goo is overall left ambiguous, and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written and interesting, in a weird way. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is well-written, but not really that interesting to me. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thought of Hooker-B-Gone made me upvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Weird, and the very premise of anomalous "oh shit gotta get rid of this dead hooker" fluid is pretty good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New SCP up. Comments, feedback and such welcome.
